label: Information Theory
shortDescription: The mathematical study of the quantification, storage, and communication of information.
modulesDescription: Read the information theory chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Information Theory for Free | CalcPath
ogDescription: Master entropy, mutual information, source and channel coding, rate-distortion theory, network information theory, and advanced topics like quantum information and Kolmogorov complexity — with detailed explanations, worked examples, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete information theory course covering foundations of entropy and information measures, source coding and data compression algorithms, channel coding and error correction, rate-distortion theory, network information theory, and advanced topics including quantum information theory, algorithmic information theory, secret sharing, and differential privacy. Features ELI5 intuition, step-by-step worked examples, common mistakes, and hundreds of practice problems.

topics.title: Information theory
topics.description: The study of quantifying, storing, and communicating information using mathematical measures of uncertainty and surprise.

topics.title: Entropy (information theory)
topics.description: The expected amount of information or uncertainty contained in a random variable, measured in bits or nats.

topics.title: Joint entropy
topics.description: The entropy of a pair of random variables considered together, quantifying total uncertainty in the joint distribution.

topics.title: Conditional entropy
topics.description: The remaining uncertainty in one random variable given knowledge of another, measuring residual information.

topics.title: Mutual information
topics.description: The amount of information one random variable contains about another, quantifying dependence and reduction in uncertainty.

topics.title: Relative entropy
topics.description: The Kullback-Leibler divergence measuring how one probability distribution diverges from a second reference distribution.

topics.title: Entropy rate
topics.description: The average entropy per symbol in a stochastic process as length goes to infinity.

topics.title: Asymptotic equipartition property
topics.description: The theorem that typical sequences from a stationary ergodic source have probability about 2^{-nH} and occupy a small set.

topics.title: Source coding theorem
topics.description: Shannon's theorem stating the fundamental limit on lossless data compression is the entropy of the source.

topics.title: Data compression
topics.description: Techniques to represent data using fewer bits by removing redundancy while allowing reconstruction.

topics.title: Huffman coding
topics.description: An optimal prefix-free variable-length code for lossless compression based on symbol probabilities.

topics.title: Arithmetic coding
topics.description: A lossless compression method that encodes an entire message as a single fractional number in [0,1).

topics.title: Lempel–Ziv–Welch algorithm
topics.description: A universal dictionary-based lossless compression algorithm used in GIF and TIFF.

topics.title: Shannon–Fano coding
topics.description: An early prefix code construction method that assigns shorter codes to more probable symbols.

topics.title: Universal code
topics.description: A prefix code that achieves compression close to entropy without knowing the source distribution in advance.

topics.title: Kolmogorov complexity
topics.description: The length of the shortest program that outputs a given string, a measure of its intrinsic complexity.

topics.title: Channel capacity
topics.description: The maximum rate at which information can be reliably transmitted over a noisy channel.

topics.title: Noisy-channel coding theorem
topics.description: Shannon's theorem proving that reliable communication is possible at rates below channel capacity.

topics.title: Error detection and correction
topics.description: Methods to detect and fix errors introduced during transmission or storage of data.

topics.title: Hamming code
topics.description: A family of linear error-correcting codes that can detect up to two-bit errors or correct one-bit errors.

topics.title: Low-density parity-check code
topics.description: Modern sparse-graph error-correcting codes with excellent performance under iterative decoding.

topics.title: Turbo code
topics.description: High-performance iterative error-correcting codes approaching the Shannon limit for noisy channels.

topics.title: Polar code
topics.description: The first explicit construction of capacity-achieving error-correcting codes with efficient encoding and decoding.

topics.title: Channel coding with feedback
topics.description: Error correction strategies when the receiver can send information back to the sender.

topics.title: Rate–distortion theory
topics.description: The branch of information theory dealing with lossy compression and the fundamental trade-off between rate and distortion.

topics.title: Quantization (signal processing)
topics.description: The process of mapping continuous or high-precision values to a finite set of discrete levels.

topics.title: Vector quantization
topics.description: Quantization of vectors rather than scalars, used in image and speech compression for better efficiency.

topics.title: Rate–distortion function
topics.description: The function R(D) giving the minimum rate needed to achieve a given average distortion D.

topics.title: Blahut–Arimoto algorithm
topics.description: An iterative algorithm for computing the rate-distortion function and channel capacity.

topics.title: Multiple-access channel
topics.description: A communication channel with multiple senders and one receiver, central to network information theory.

topics.title: Broadcast channel
topics.description: A channel with one sender and multiple receivers, each getting a possibly different noisy version.

topics.title: Network coding
topics.description: A technique where nodes in a network combine incoming packets before forwarding to increase throughput.

topics.title: Slepian–Wolf coding
topics.description: Distributed lossless compression of correlated sources with separate encoders but joint decoder.

topics.title: Wyner–Ziv coding
topics.description: Lossy compression with side information available only at the decoder.

topics.title: Quantum information theory
topics.description: The extension of information theory to quantum systems using qubits and quantum channels.

topics.title: Algorithmic information theory
topics.description: The study of information content via Kolmogorov complexity and algorithmic probability.

topics.title: Kolmogorov complexity
topics.description: Further exploration of Kolmogorov complexity in the context of advanced and algorithmic topics.

topics.title: Rate distortion with side information
topics.description: Lossy source coding where side information is available to the encoder or decoder.

topics.title: Secret sharing
topics.description: Cryptographic methods to distribute a secret among participants such that only qualified subsets can reconstruct it.

topics.title: Secure multiparty computation
topics.description: Protocols allowing parties to jointly compute a function over private inputs without revealing them.

topics.title: Differential privacy
topics.description: A formal mathematical definition of privacy that quantifies the risk of individual data leakage.

topics.title: Common randomness
topics.description: The generation and extraction of shared random bits between parties, often over noisy channels.
